skill.name: notion-knowledge-capture
skill.description: Turn conversations, notes, and decisions into structured Notion pages with links, owners, summaries, and database properties.
skill.summary: Capture working knowledge from chats and notes into organized Notion documentation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, file names, and ordinary wording in SKILL.md. The confirmed concern is legitimate but sensitive Notion MCP usage: the skill instructs setup of a remote Notion MCP and then fetches, creates, and updates Notion workspace content.

medium_findings.title: Remote Notion MCP Access to Workspace Content
medium_findings.description: The skill directs setup of a remote Notion MCP endpoint, OAuth login, and Notion operations that search, fetch, create, and update workspace pages. This is expected for the skill, but it can expose or alter sensitive Notion content if used with broad workspace permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names Notion MCP setup, OAuth login, search, fetch, create, and update operations. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is permission and data exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution patterns are Markdown inline code references to Notion tools, Codex commands, configuration keys, and reference file names. No Ruby code, shell script, or dynamic command execution logic is present in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the backticks are Markdown formatting, not executable Ruby syntax. The file is a 57-line instruction document with no embedded script block.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives for Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not correspond to cryptographic operations in the reviewed lines. The referenced lines contain the skill description, metadata text, a workflow heading, and database guide references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no hashing, encryption, random generation, or crypto library calls. The scanner likely matched substrings in prose or file names.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Setup Commands for Remote MCP
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells users or agents to run Codex MCP setup commands and enable the remote MCP client. These commands are understandable for Notion integration, but they change local tool configuration and connect the agent to an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The setup commands are explicit and target the official Notion MCP URL. The commands are not hidden or obfuscated, but they still alter agent connectivity and authentication state.

content.value_statement: Teams lose useful decisions and context when they stay buried in chat. This skill turns conversations and notes into structured Notion pages with summaries, links, owners, and database fields.
actual_capabilities: Classifies captured material as a decision, how-to, FAQ, wiki entry, learning note, or documentation page.
Searches and fetches existing Notion pages before creating or updating related content.
Drafts structured pages with summaries, context, source links, tags, owners, and status fields.
Creates or updates Notion pages through Notion MCP tools when the integration is connected.
Adds relations, backlinks, changelog notes, and follow-up tasks when the source material requires them.
limitations: Requires a connected Notion MCP server and OAuth login before workspace operations can run.
Depends on available Notion database schemas and may need user confirmation when multiple destinations match.
Can only capture information present in the conversation, notes, or accessible Notion pages.
May create or update sensitive workspace content, so permissions should be scoped carefully.

use_cases.title: Create Decision Records
use_cases.description: Convert a planning discussion into a Notion decision page with alternatives, rationale, owners, and links.

use_cases.title: Build Team Wiki Entries
use_cases.description: Turn repeated chat explanations into reusable wiki pages with context, tags, and related records.

use_cases.title: Document Support Knowledge
use_cases.description: Transform customer questions or support notes into FAQ entries with concise answers and links.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a Basic Note
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner capture from a short conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Capture this conversation as a Notion wiki entry. Include a clear title, summary, key facts, tags, and source links.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Decision Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this discussion into a Notion decision log entry. Capture the decision, alternatives, rationale, owner, status, and follow-up actions.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Notion for related documentation, fetch the best match, and update it with the new details from these notes. Preserve existing useful context.

prompt_templates.title: Connect Related Knowledge
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or update the right Notion pages from this project conversation. Add relations to hub pages, related docs, decisions, and any follow-up tasks.

output_examples.input: A team discussion explains why onboarding emails should move from manual sends to an automated sequence.
output_examples.output: A Notion decision page with a summary, selected approach, rejected options, rationale, owner, status, and links to related onboarding docs.

output_examples.input: Several chat messages answer the same setup question for a new internal tool.
output_examples.output: A Notion FAQ entry with a direct question, concise answer, prerequisites, troubleshooting notes, and references to deeper setup documentation.

output_examples.input: Meeting notes describe a recurring deployment process and its edge cases.
output_examples.output: A Notion how-to page with steps, prerequisites, warnings, owner, tags, and backlinks to the deployment hub.

best_practices: Confirm the target audience and content type before creating a page.
Search existing Notion pages first to avoid duplicate knowledge records.
Use the least privileged Notion access needed for the databases being updated.
anti_patterns: Do not publish private chat content without checking whether it belongs in Notion.
Do not create duplicate pages when an existing page should be updated.
Do not connect Notion MCP with broader workspace permissions than the task requires.

faq.question: What does this skill create in Notion?
faq.answer: It creates or updates wiki entries, how-to guides, FAQs, decision logs, learning notes, and documentation pages.

faq.question: Does it need Notion MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects a connected Notion MCP server and OAuth login for Notion search, fetch, create, and update actions.

faq.question: Can it update existing pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to search and fetch existing pages before editing related documentation.

faq.question: Does it decide the database automatically?
faq.answer: It selects a matching database from reference guidance, but asks the user when multiple destinations are possible.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive workspaces?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill can read and modify Notion content, so workspace permissions should be limited to relevant databases.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with Notion actions depending on available MCP tooling.
